articles.full_text:
Mercury to rise in Himachal, heatwave alerts in 3 districts
Meteorological Centre Shimla issues yellow warning for Una, Bilaspur and Solan districts for June 9 and 10, resulting in extensive heat wave conditions
Himachal Pradesh is set to witness scorching heat from June 9 onwards, as the Meteorological Centre, Shimla, has issued a yellow alert for heat wave conditions in many parts of the state.
As per the centre, a yellow warning has been issued for Una, Bilaspur and Solan districts for June 9 and 10, resulting in extensive heat wave conditions in isolated places of these districts.
As a result, minimum temperatures across the state are expected to surge by 2°C to 4°C, while maximum temperatures are expected to rise by 3°C to 5°C. Additionally, the centre has also issued a yellow weather warning for heavy rains across the state on June 11.
Meanwhile, very light precipitation was witnessed in several parts of the state during the last 24 hours, while the weather was mostly dry in the state.
Minimum temperatures across the state were normal and ranged between 5°C and 26°C. However, maximum temperatures across the state surged by 2°C to 5°C and ranged between 19°C and 41°C.
Maximum temperature in Shimla was 27.4°C, Dharamshala 34°C, Manali 27.2°C, Kangra 37°C, Mandi 36.2°C, Sundernagar 36.6°C, Kufri 20.7°C, Kalpa 22.8°C, Keylong 20°C, Nahan 34.6°C, Bhuntar 34.4°C and Chamba 32.8°C.
With 41°C maximum temperatures, Una was the hottest place in the state, while Tabo in Lahaul and Spiti was the coldest as it recorded a 5.2°C minimum temperature.

articles.full_text:
Heatwave alert in three Himachal dists on June 9, 10; relief likely from June 11
The weather office predicted maximum temperatures would gradually rise by 3-5 degrees in many parts of the state over the next 3-4 days, while minimum temperatures are likely to rise by 2-4 degrees
With mercury expected to rise in Himachal Pradesh in coming days, the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Shimla, office has sounded a yellow alert of heatwave conditions in Una, Bilaspur and Solan districts on June 9 and 10.
The weather office predicted maximum temperatures would gradually rise by 3-5 degrees in many parts of the state over the next 3-4 days, while minimum temperatures are likely to rise by 2-4 degrees.
Dry weather is expected to prevail in the state’s plains and middle hills on June 8 and 9, while light showers are likely at isolated places in the higher reaches. Dry weather is likely to continue in plain areas on June 10.
On Sunday, Una was the hottest in the state, recording a maximum temperature of 41 degrees Celsius. It was followed by maximum temperatures of 38.7 degrees recorded in Neri and 37 degrees recorded in Kangra.
Shimla recorded a maximum temperature of 27.4 degrees, which was 1.6 degrees above normal. A maximum temperature of 34 degrees was recorded in Dharamshala, 1.5 degrees above normal. Manali recorded a maximum temperature of 27.2 degrees.
Relief likely from June 11
From June 11 to 13, light showers are expected across the state. According to IMD, a fresh western disturbance is likely to affect Western Himalayan Region from June 11.
For June 11, the IMD has sounded a yellow alert for thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds across all districts of the state except Kinnaur.
During the last 24 hours, light rain was observed at isolated places over the state, with thunderstorms and lightning reported in Jot, Sundernagar and Kangra.
